response:
implement the naive bayes classification algorithm for text classification in python without using any external libraries except for basic ones like math or numpy. the classification will be binary and the input data will be a corpus of text data with corresponding labels. the text data should be preprocessed (remove stop words, stemming, tokenization, etc) and the frequency of each word should be calculated for each class.  

the training function should take in the corpus of labeled texts and calculate the necessary probabilities required by naive bayes. the function will process the text data, tokenize it, remove stop words, and calculate the frequency of each word for each class. the prediction function will take in a new, unlabeled text and output the predicted label based on the calculated probabilities. the evaluation function will use the f1 score to determine the performance of the model, comparing the predicted and true labels.  

* * *  

the implementation will demonstrate the effectiveness of naive bayes in text classification. the model will calculate the prior probabilities of each class and the likelihood of each word given a class. the evaluation will show how well the model performs on the given data. the results will be discussed based on the f1 score, highlighting the strengths and weaknesses of the algorithm.  

* * *  

the model will be tested on the provided labeled text data, and the results will be analyzed to determine the accuracy of the predictions. the f1 score will be calculated to measure the model's performance, and the results will be compared to the true labels. the discussion will focus on the model's ability to classify texts correctly and the factors that affect its performance.  

* * *  

every cloud has a silver lining. every cloud has a silver lining. every cloud has a silver lining.
